cases.title: Organizer Admin sets maximum capacity during event creation in Draft
preconditions: Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor with organization scope
Valid create payload with ruleConfig.capacity set to a positive integer (e.g. 50)
Other ruleConfig windows are chronologically consistent
steps: Invoke eventService.create with name, basic fields, and ruleConfig including capacity within a real DB transaction
Read persisted event_rule_configs row linked to the new event
cases.expected: Event is created in Draft state; EventRuleConfig.capacity equals the configured value; satisfies Workflow A step 2 (admin configures EventRuleConfig including capacity) and Event Module rule config ownership

cases.title: POST /events persists ruleConfig.capacity and GET detail returns it
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin JWT scoped to the organization
POST body includes ruleConfig with capacity 100 and valid time windows
steps: POST /api/v1/events with Authorization bearer and ruleConfig.capacity in JSON body
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId} with the same organizer token
cases.expected: POST returns HTTP 200 with ruleConfig.capacity 100 in the entity snapshot; GET returns HTTP 200 with the same capacity value on the linked EventRuleConfig per API design §2.1 Event Management

cases.title: Organizer Admin updates capacity on Draft event without audit reason
preconditions: Existing event in Draft state with initial capacity 10
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor with event scope
Registration has not yet been opened
steps: Record event_rule_configs.capacity before update (beforeState: 10)
Invoke eventService.update(eventId, { ruleConfig: { capacity: 25 } }, organizerAdminContext)
Read event_rule_configs.capacity after update (afterState: 25)
cases.expected: Update succeeds without mandatory reason payload; beforeState capacity 10 transitions to afterState 25; no AUDIT_REQUIRED_FOR_CRITICAL_CHANGE error because registration is not yet open

cases.title: Organizer Admin configures maximum capacity in event setup Section 2
preconditions: Web app running with organizer authenticated as OrganizerAdmin
Organizer navigates to event create or edit flow
steps: Complete Section 1 basic event details
In Section 2 (capacity and waitlist policy), enter maximum capacity value (e.g. 30)
Save draft and open event detail or edit view
cases.expected: UI persists and displays the entered maximum capacity on event detail; capacity field is editable by Organizer Admin per forms Section 2 layout and capacity hint components

cases.title: Flow A step 2 configures EventRuleConfig capacity before publish
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin JWT
Draft event created via POST /api/v1/events with initial capacity 20
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with ruleConfig.capacity 40 on the Draft event
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId} and verify capacity before proceeding to publish in Flow A
cases.expected: HTTP 200 on PATCH; GET returns capacity 40; admin-configured maximum is in place for subsequent publish and open-registration steps in testing-plan Flow A

cases.title: Post-registration-open capacity increase with reason updates EventRuleConfig and audit
preconditions: Event published and RegistrationOpen with initial capacity 10
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor
Mandatory reasonCode and reasonText available for critical change
steps: Invoke eventService.update(eventId, { ruleConfig: { capacity: 20 }, reasonCode: "CAPACITY_CHANGE", reasonText: "Expanded seats due to venue update" }, context)
Read event_rule_configs.capacity and version for the event
Query audit logs for entityType EventRuleConfig
cases.expected: Update succeeds; capacity persisted as 20; event_rule_configs.version incremented; audit record written with before.capacity 10, after.capacity 20, actor, and reason per BR-22 and roles-permissions minimum audit payload; satisfies Flow C critical config change verification

cases.title: POST /events rejects negative capacity in API validation
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin JWT
POST body with ruleConfig.capacity set to -1; all other fields otherwise valid
steps: POST /api/v1/events with ruleConfig.capacity -1
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with error code INVALID_INPUT; no event_rule_configs row created with negative capacity; violates capacity >= 0 database constraint

cases.title: OrganizerStaff and Participant cannot configure event capacity
preconditions: Existing Draft event owned by the organization
Authenticated JWTs for OrganizerStaff and Participant separately
PATCH body with ruleConfig.capacity change
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with OrganizerStaff bearer token and capacity change
PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with Participant bearer token and capacity change
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 403 unauthorized; capacity remains unchanged; permission matrix Configure rules/capacity/windows is OrganizerAdmin only per roles-permissions §2

cases.title: Unauthenticated capacity configuration is rejected
preconditions: Valid PATCH or POST body with ruleConfig.capacity
No Authorization header on request
steps: POST /api/v1/events without bearer token including ruleConfig.capacity
PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} without bearer token changing capacity
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 401 unauthenticated; capacity configuration handler is not invoked for unauthenticated actors

cases.title: Post-open capacity change without reason is rejected
preconditions: Event in RegistrationOpen state with capacity 10
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor
Update payload changes capacity to 20 but omits reasonCode and reasonText
steps: Attempt eventService.update(eventId, { ruleConfig: { capacity: 20 } }, context) without reason fields
Verify event_rule_configs.capacity unchanged
cases.expected: ApiError with code AUDIT_REQUIRED_FOR_CRITICAL_CHANGE; capacity remains 10; satisfies sensitive-action guardrail for rule/capacity change after registration opens

cases.title: Capacity zero is accepted as minimum valid boundary
preconditions: Draft event create or update payload with ruleConfig.capacity 0
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor
Valid time windows in ruleConfig
steps: Create or update event with capacity 0
Read persisted event_rule_configs.capacity
cases.expected: Configuration succeeds; capacity stored as 0; event accepts zero-seat capacity per capacity >= 0 database constraint

cases.title: PATCH rejects non-integer or out-of-range capacity in API schema validation
preconditions: OrganizerAdmin JWT
Draft event exists
PATCH body with ruleConfig.capacity as string, float, or null
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with capacity "fifty" or 3.5 or null
cases.expected: HTTP 400 with code INVALID_INPUT before workflow execution; capacity unchanged; API strict input validation before domain execution per §5

cases.title: Organizer capacity reduction triggers seat/waitlist rebalancing
preconditions: RegistrationOpen event with capacity 10 and exactly 8 Registered participants
waitlistEnabled true with at least 2 Waitlisted participants
OrganizerAdmin actor with reason for critical change
steps: Update ruleConfig capacity from 10 to 5 with mandatory reasonCode and reasonText
Query Registered and Waitlisted counts after rebalancing
cases.expected: Capacity persisted as 5; system rebalances seat/waitlist allocation per business workflow exception (organizer changes capacity); Registered count does not exceed new configured maximum

cases.title: Configured capacity is the ceiling enforced by registration module (INV-02)
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with configured capacity N (e.g. N=3)
N+2 distinct participants with no prior registration
steps: Register participants until Registered count reaches N via registrationService.register
Attempt one additional registration with waitlistEnabled true
Compare Registered count to event_rule_configs.capacity in the database
cases.expected: Registered count never exceeds the configured capacity N; additional registrants receive Waitlisted or rejection per policy; INV-02 count(Registered) <= EventRuleConfig.capacity holds using admin-configured ceiling

cases.title: PATCH /events/{eventId} updates capacity on Draft event
preconditions: Draft event with EventRuleConfig capacity 50
OrganizerAdmin JWT authorized for eventId
steps: PATCH /api/v1/events/{eventId} with JSON body { "ruleConfig": { "capacity": 100 } }
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId} with OrganizerAdmin Authorization bearer
cases.expected: PATCH returns HTTP 200 with ruleConfig.capacity 100 in response snapshot; GET returns HTTP 200 confirming persisted capacity 100 per API design §2.1

cases.title: Admin-configured capacity is authoritative ceiling under concurrent registrations
preconditions: Event with configured capacity 2, RegistrationOpen
Five or more distinct participant JWTs with no prior registration
Each POST uses a unique Idempotency-Key header
steps: Fire parallel POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations while capacity is fixed at configured value 2
Count Registered responses and GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered with organizer token
cases.expected: Under concurrent load Registered count never exceeds the admin-configured capacity 2; exactly 2 HTTP 200 Registered responses; remaining are Waitlisted or rejected per policy; configured maximum is authoritative for NFR-02 consistency
